subject: Re: Warp crashes after initiating template matching
body:
Dear Anthony,

template matching hasn't received much attention since the launch, so the 
user experience is not yet on par with the rest of Warp. Could you please 
describe the settings you used, and the dimensions of the template?

Unrelated to the bug, I'm wondering if you could describe the use case that 
favored template matching over picking with a re-trained BoxNet? I'd just 
pick everything unspecifically with BoxNet, and then classify with higher 
precision in cryoSPARC or RELION, but perhaps I'm missing an important 
application where this wouldn't make sense?

Cheers,
Dimitry


On Tuesday, August 14, 2018 at 8:51:03 PM UTC+2, Anthony Nguyen wrote:
>
> Dear Dimitry,
>
> I'm having trouble running the template matching function in Warp. 
> Essentially, the program crashes when the first mic is about to finish and 
> gives this error in the lastcrash.txt file. I'm not quite sure what's 
> happening, and any help would be appreciated. 
>
> Thanks,
> Anthony
>
> __________________________________________________________________________
>
> 8/14/2018 2:27:30 PM:
> System.Exception: cudaErrorIllegalAddress
>    at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions()
>    at Warp.Movie.MatchFull(Image originalStack, ProcessingOptionsFullMatch 
> options, Image template, Func`4 progressCallback)
>    at 
> Warp.Controls.TaskDialogs.TwoD.Dialog2DMatch.<>c__DisplayClass18_1.<ButtonMatch_OnClick>b__4(Movie 
> item, Int32 gpuID)
>    at Warp.Tools.Helper.<>c__DisplayClass41_2`1.<ForEachGPU>b__0()
>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.RunInternal(ExecutionContext 
> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
> preserveSyncCtx)
>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
> preserveSyncCtx)
>    at System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state)
>    at System.Threading.ThreadHelper.ThreadStart()
>
>
